alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian approach effectively identifies optimal transformations that map model input points to low dimensional latent variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian approach identifies optimal transformations that map input points to low dimensional latent variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim restates the preprint claim with effectively identical wording and intent about identifying transformations to low-dimensional latent variables.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework employs a Geodesic Monte Carlo sampling algorithm to account for orthogonality constraints on the projection matrix.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the Geodesic Monte Carlo algorithm or orthogonality-handling detail, so this implementation-specific claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework is validated on three synthetic problems with a known lower-dimensional subspace.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework is validated on three synthetic problems with a known lower-dimensional subspace.
alignments.rationale: The validation on three synthetic problems is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework demonstrates benefits in the aerodynamic optimization of a last-stage blade for an industrial gas turbine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework illustrates benefits in the computationally challenging aerodynamic optimization of a last-stage blade for an industrial gas turbine.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same result but adds the qualifier "computationally challenging," which clarifies the context without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Gaussian Processes regression (GPs) is a robust meta-modeling technique that allows for fast model prediction and exploration of response surfaces.
alignments.rationale: This general statement about Gaussian Process regression does not appear in the preprint and is newly stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adiabatic quantum computers like the D-Wave 2000Q can approximately solve NP-hard optimization problems faster than classical computers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adiabatic quantum computers can approximately solve NP-hard problems, such as the quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO), faster than classical computers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level claim about adiabatic quantum computers solving NP-hard/QUBO problems faster than classical machines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adiabatic quantum computers might be instrumental in training machine learning models efficiently in the post Moore's law era.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adiabatic quantum computers might be instrumental in training machine learning models efficiently in the post Moore’s law era.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this forward-looking conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training problems of linear regression, support vector machine (SVM), and equal-sized k-means clustering can be formulated as QUBO problems for efficient training on adiabatic quantum computers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training problems of three machine learning models—linear regression, support vector machine (SVM) and balanced k-means clustering—can be formulated as QUBO problems.
alignments.rationale: Both documents claim these three training problems can be formulated as QUBO; wording differences are minor ("equal-sized" vs "balanced").

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The time and space complexities of our formulations for SVM and equal-sized k-means clustering are better, and for linear regression are equivalent, to their classical counterparts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The time and space complexities of our formulations are better (in case of SVM and balanced k-means clustering) or equivalent (in case of linear regression) to their classical counterparts.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same complexity comparisons, with only minor wording changes ("equal-sized" vs "balanced").

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel neuro-robotics model is capable of counting real items.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel neuro-robotics model is capable of counting real items.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained model can count a set of items while simultaneously pointing to them.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained model can count a set of items while simultaneously pointing to them.
alignments.rationale: The result about simultaneous counting and pointing is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The influence of pointing on the counting process was investigated and compared with results from studies with children.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The influence of pointing on the counting process was investigated and compared with studies involving children.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the investigation of pointing effects and the comparison with child studies; wording is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The obtained results on counting performance show similarities with those from human studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The counting performance of the model shows similarities with results from human studies.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in content, but the publication lowers certainty from high to medium, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unsupervised learning algorithm identifies the conformational transition with greater than 95% accuracy in most cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised learning algorithm identifies the conformational transition with greater than 95% accuracy in most cases.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptation of the naive Bayes classifier establishes a new connection to information entropy that allows variation in structural detail without affecting categorization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptation of the naïve Bayes classifier establishes a new connection to information entropy that allows variation in structural detail without affecting categorization.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved verbatim, aside from minor typographic differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method's derivation from Bayesian analysis on inter-atomic contacts facilitates understanding and extension to more complex cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method's derivation from Bayesian analysis on inter-atomic contacts facilitates understanding and extension to more complex cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated analysis of RFP responses through Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems can reduce time and effort needed to explore historical responses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated analysis of RFP responses through Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems can reduce time and effort needed to explore historical responses.
alignments.rationale: The result about time/effort reduction is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel Bi-LSTM based regression model that provides deeper insight into phrases which latently impact scoring of responses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel Bi-LSTM and a transformer based regression model for scoring RFP responses.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the methodological claim by adding a transformer-based model in addition to the Bi-LSTM, changing the claimed scope of proposed models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our methodology demonstrates the merits of using extensive quantitative experiments to assess RFP scoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our methodology provides deeper insight into phrases that latently impact the scoring of responses.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes phrase-level insight rather than the preprint's emphasis on extensive quantitative experiments, indicating a shift in emphasis and a narrower focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We qualitatively assess the impact of important phrases using human evaluators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We qualitatively assess the impact of important phrases using human evaluators.
alignments.rationale: The qualitative human-evaluator assessment claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduce a novel problem statement that can be used to further improve the state of the art in NLP based automatic scoring systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a novel problem statement that can be used to further improve the state of the art in NLP based automatic scoring systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the novel problem-statement claim but presents it with reduced certainty (preprint: high; publication: medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can extract and interpret measurements from multi-dimensional FLIM data sets with substantial improvement in speed over conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can extract and interpret measurements from multi-dimensional FLIM data sets with substantial improvement in speed over conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are effectively identical in content and emphasis, showing no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lifetime extraction strategies using machine learning result in higher accuracy in classifying and segmenting FLIM images compared to conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lifetime extraction strategies using machine learning result in higher accuracy in classifying and segmenting FLIM images compared to conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim between preprint and publication with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are two potential directions to improve FLIM with machine learning, supported by proof of concept demonstrations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are two potential directions to improve FLIM with machine learning, supported by proof of concept demonstrations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement is retained exactly in the publication, with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adiabatic quantum computing approach for training a linear regression model demonstrates up to 2.8x speedup over the classical approach on larger datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantum approach attains up to 2.8× speedup over the classical approach on larger datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same quantitative speedup claim and context; wording changes are minor and do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantum approach performs at par with the classical approach on the regression error metric. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantum approach performs at par with the classical approach on the regression error metric. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance-on-metric claim is verbatim and unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adiabatic quantum computers present a promising alternative to improve machine learning training times. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adiabatic quantum computers present a promising alternative to improve machine learning training times. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about promise and applicability is carried over without change in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results obtained in this work must be interpreted within the context of the specific hardware and software implementations of these machines. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation/interpretation guidance is newly stated in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for 3D object recognition using light curtains is resource-efficient and controllable. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method for 3D object recognition using light curtains is proposed, which is a resource-efficient controllable sensor. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved with equivalent meaning; publication rephrases but does not change substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction uncertainty of a deep learning based 3D point cloud detector can guide active perception. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction uncertainty of a deep learning based 3D point cloud detector is used to guide active perception. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from a proposed use to an asserted use (result), clarifying the claim while retaining the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An optimization objective derived from a neural network's uncertainty can maximize information gain when placing light curtains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel optimization algorithm is developed to optimally place light curtains to maximize coverage of uncertain regions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's objective-focused claim is rendered in the publication as an implemented algorithm achieving the stated aim, which clarifies (and slightly reframes) the original point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel optimization algorithm has been developed to maximize the objective by encoding physical constraints into a constraint graph. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Efficient optimization is achieved by encoding the physical constraints of the device into a constraint graph, which is optimized with dynamic programming. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an implementation detail (dynamic programming) and frames the algorithm as achieving efficient optimization, clarifying the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sequentially placing uncertainty-guided light curtains can improve detection accuracy of a 3D detector. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A 3D detector can be trained to detect objects in a scene by sequentially placing uncertainty-guided light curtains to successively improve detection accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the sequential-placement result with stronger wording (training context and higher certainty), thus clarifying and increasing confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical foundations of a metric temporal extension of Answer Set Programming are elaborated upon. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A metric temporal extension of Answer Set Programming is developed based on the logic of Here-and-There and its non-monotonic extension, Equilibrium Logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the precise logical bases (Here-and-There and Equilibrium Logic), elaborating the theoretical foundation stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed logic is based on the same semantic underpinnings as previous extensions of ASP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new logic is built on the same semantic foundations as previous extensions of ASP with constructs from Linear Temporal and Dynamic Logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies which constructs from temporal/dynamic logics are involved while keeping the same foundational claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple time domain of bounded time steps is utilized in the development of the logic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a simple time domain of bounded time steps allows for a uniform comparison of all variants of the logic. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication links the choice of time domain to its consequence (enabling uniform comparison), clarifying the original technical detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework allows for the comparison of all variants in a uniform manner. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a simple time domain of bounded time steps allows for a uniform comparison of all variants of the logic. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication connects the uniform-comparison conclusion specifically to the time-domain choice, clarifying the mechanism behind the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variants can ultimately be combined in a common implementation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework developed enables the combination of different variants in a common implementation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's conclusion with slightly clearer wording about the framework enabling combination, preserving intent and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed task offloading method based on meta reinforcement learning can adapt fast to new environments with a small number of gradient updates and samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed task offloading method based on meta reinforcement learning can adapt fast to new environments with a small number of gradient updates and samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced exactly and retains the same emphasis on fast adaptation and sample/gradient efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that this new offloading method can reduce the latency by up to 25% compared to three baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that this new offloading method can reduce the latency by up to 25 percent compared to three baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and comparison to baselines are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing deep reinforcement learning methods for task offloading have weak adaptability to new environments due to low sample efficiency and the need for full retraining. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing deep reinforcement learning-based methods have weak adaptability to new environments due to low sample efficiency and the need for full retraining. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is restated with equivalent meaning and emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method improves detection precision (mAP) by utilizing prior knowledge about scene geometry. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method improves detection precision (mAP) by utilizing prior knowledge about scene geometry. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The texts are effectively identical in claim content; only the label moved from 'contribution' to 'conclusion', so the semantic content, scope, and expressed confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method predicts the spatial scale for each pixel of the input frame based on the robot's dimensions and camera inclination angles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method predicts the spatial scale for each pixel of the input frame based on the robot's dimensions and camera inclination angles. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same, but the reported certainty is reduced from high to medium in the publication, so the claim is semantically unchanged but its confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Detection supplemented by the scale channel outperforms standard RGB-based detection with small computational overhead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Detection supplemented by the scale channel outperforms standard RGB-based detection with minimal computational overhead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines wording from 'small' to 'minimal' computational overhead (a stronger wording), so the claim is clarified and slightly amplified in its confidence about computational cost while maintaining the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning model effectively detects and segments pneumonia in chest X-ray images, specifically for COVID-19 pneumonia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model demonstrates the ability to simultaneously detect and localize pneumonia in chest X-ray images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from 'segments' to the weaker term 'localize' and omits explicit mention of segmentation and COVID-19 specificity, reducing the specificity and strength of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'Pneumonia Ratio' calculated from segmentations indicates the severity of the disease. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'Pneumonia Ratio' calculated from localization maps indicates disease severity in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The measurement of disease severity allows for the construction of a disease extent profile over time for hospitalized COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assessment of disease severity contributes to building a temporal disease extent profile for hospitalized patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation strategy using Digital Reconstructed Radiographs (DRRs) demonstrates the model's relevance to patient monitoring by comparing disease progression profiles from DRRs and CT volumes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation strategy involving Digital Reconstructed Radiographs allows for comparison of disease progression profiles generated from synthetic X-ray and CT volumes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Point Proposal Network (PPN) is a point source detector that utilises deep convolutional neural networks for fast source detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Point Proposal Network (PPN) is a point source detector that utilizes deep convolutional neural networks for fast source detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical between preprint and publication; only minor spelling variation ('utilises' vs 'utilizes') is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PPN performs source detection faster than leading alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPN performs source detection faster than leading alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PPN is able to scale to large images, unlike alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPN is able to scale to large images, unlike alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PPN is less precise when compared to leading alternative approaches. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPN is less precise when compared to leading alternative approaches. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The limitation/result labeling changed but the substantive claim about lower precision is identical, so semantic content and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dimension-adaptive pooling (DAP) layer enhances the flexibility and robustness of deep neural networks (DNNs) to changes in sensor availability and sampling rates. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dimension-adaptive pooling (DAP) layer enhances the flexibility and robustness of deep neural networks (DNNs) to changes in sensor availability and sampling rates. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dimension-adaptive training (DAT) procedure enables DNNs using DAP to better generalize over feasible data dimensions at inference time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining DAP and DAT transforms non-adaptive DNNs into a Dimension-Adaptive Neural Architecture (DANA) without increasing the number of parameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining DAP and DAT transforms existing non-adaptive DNNs into a Dimension-Adaptive Neural Architecture (DANA) without increasing the number of parameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DANA provides better classification accuracy across a range of data dimensions at inference time compared to existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DANA provides better average classification accuracy over a range of possible data dimensions at inference time compared to existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DANA prevents significant losses in classification accuracy of state-of-the-art DNNs and better captures correlated patterns in sensor data under dynamic conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DANA prevents significant losses in classification accuracy of state-of-the-art DNNs and better captures correlated patterns in sensor data under dynamic conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural program models often fail to generalize their performance even with small semantically preserving changes to the programs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural program models often fail to generalize their performance even with small semantically preserving changes to the programs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural program models based on data and control dependencies in programs generalize better than those based only on abstract syntax trees. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural program models based on data and control dependencies in programs generalize better than those based only on abstract syntax trees (ASTs). (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalizability of correct predictions produced by neural program models can be improved as the size of the training dataset grows and diversifies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalizability of correct predictions produced by neural program models can be improved as the size of the training dataset grows and diversifies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results on the generalizability of neural program models provide insights to measure their limitations and offer a foundation for their improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results on the generalizability of neural program models provide insights to measure their limitations and offer a basis for their improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper extends the definition of program completion to programs with input and output in a subset of the input language of the ASP grounder gringo. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper extends the definition of program completion to programs with input and output in a subset of the input language of the ASP grounder gringo. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between stable models and completion is studied in the context of the extended definition. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary experiments with the software tools anthem and vampire are described for verifying the correctness of programs with input and output. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proofs of theorems are based on a lemma that relates the semantics of programs studied in this paper to stable models of first-order formulas. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proofs of theorems are based on a lemma that relates the semantics of programs studied in this paper to stable models of first-order formulas. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a method for formal verification of Answer Set Programming (ASP) that ensures the answer sets of a logic program correspond to the solutions of the encoded problem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents a method for formal verification of Answer Set Programming (ASP) that ensures the answer sets of a logic program correspond to the solutions of the encoded problem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A formal specification language based on ASP modules is introduced, allowing for isolated proofs of informal aspects of problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed specification language includes a novel definition of program modules that can incorporate local hidden atoms at different levels. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed specification language is based on a novel definition of program modules that can include local hidden atoms at various levels. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Kernel Minimum Regularized Covariance Determinant (KMRCD) estimator is not restricted to elliptical data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed kernel minimum regularized covariance determinant (KMRCD) estimator is not restricted to elliptical data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KMRCD algorithm addresses the issues of non-elliptical data and increased computation time with many variables. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A fast algorithm is constructed for the KMRCD that utilizes the kernel trick to speed up computations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A fast algorithm is constructed for the KMRCD that exploits the kernel trick to speed up computations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KMRCD algorithm performs well in simulations and is effective on real-life data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KMRCD algorithm performs well in simulations and is illustrated on real-life data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants do not only focus on accuracy when assessing ML systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
